name: Pete's White Pizza
recipeIngredient: 2 cups firmly packed chopped fresh basil leaves
recipeInstructions:
Prepare Toppings: Place the sun-dried tomatoes in a small bowl and cover with hot tap water. Let soak, stirring occasionally, for 30 minutes. Drain and pat dry. Return to the bowl and add the red wine, balsamic vinegar, and olive oil. Let sit, stirring occasionally, at least 1 hour.
Roast the head of garlic in an oven preheated to 400F for about 30 minutes. Remove from foil and refrigerate until cool, about 30 minutes. Once cooled, carefully peel cloves and slice into halves or thirds. Set aside.
Make Dough: In a stand mixer fitted with a dough hook, combine the sugar, yeast, cornmeal, salt, and 1 cup of flour. Mix on medium-low speed for 15 seconds. Combine water and oil in a separate bowl. Add liquid ingredients to the motor while it runs, slowly. Incorporate remaining 2/3 cup flour in thirds, mixing until stiff dough forms that pulls away from the bowl sides. Knead for 2 minutes more. Cover with plastic wrap and let rise at room temperature for 1 hour, or until doubled in size.
While the dough is rising, prepare the pesto: Add garlic, Parmesan cheese, salt, and pine nuts to a food processor or blender and process until coarsely chopped and well mixed. Incorporate olive oil slowly while continuing to process until a thick paste forms. Add half of the remaining olive oil and pulse until all basil is finely chopped but not pureed. Transfer pesto to a bowl and stir in the remaining oil.
Assembly: Preheat oven and baking stone to 450F. When ready, punch down the dough and roll it out on a lightly floured surface into a thin disk about 16-18 inches in diameter. Fold the outer edge inward by about 1/2 inch and press to seal. Spread pesto evenly over the crust. Arrange sun-dried tomatoes (skin side up) and roasted garlic slices evenly across the pizza, then top with Fontina slices, evenly spaced. Sprinkle a little cornmeal on the baking stone. Carefully transfer the pizza onto the stone and reduce heat to 400F. Bake until the crust is golden and cheese is brown and bubbly, about 20 minutes. Allow to cool for a few minutes before slicing. Serves 4 as an entree.
